channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump signs dozens of executive orders, fulfilling many but not all campaign promises
item.description: Hours after his inauguration, President Trump, in front of thousands of supporters jammed into a Washington D.C.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants to pull the US out of the World Health Organization again. Here's what may happen next
item.description: President Donald Trump has used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from the World Health Organization for the second time in less than five years.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stefanik will face questions about wars and nukes at confirmation hearing to be UN ambassador
item.description: Rep. Elise Stefanik is likely to face questions at her confirmation hearing to become the U.S. ambassador to the United Nations about her lack of foreign policy experience, her strong support for Israel and her views on funding the U.N. The fourth-ranking member of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Farnoush Amiri And Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump pulls security clearance of 51 national security officials
item.description: The officials previously wrote a letter claiming that Hunter Biden's laptop had "all the classic earmarks of a Russian information operation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nancy Pelosi slams Trump’s ‘shameful’ pardons of Jan 6 defendants
item.description: Pelosi calls to remember law enforcement 'heroes' who 'ensured that democracy survived'
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs death penalty order directing attorney general to help states get lethal injection drugs
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed a sweeping execution order on the death penalty that directs the attorney general to “take all necessary and lawful action” to ensure that states have enough lethal injection drugs to carry out executions.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: President Trump discovers letter from former President Biden in Resolute Desk
item.description: President Donald Trump found a letter from former President Biden in a drawer of the Resolute Desk in the Oval Office of the White House on Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden pardons his siblings and their spouses on his way out of the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joe Biden pardoned his siblings and their spouses on his way out of the White House, saying Monday that his family had been “subjected to unrelenting attacks and threats, motivated solely by a desire to hurt me — the worst kind of partisan politics.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, COLLEEN LONG and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Immigration top priority for Trump and Florida lawmakers
item.description: Solving the illegal immigration crisis is a priority for both President Donald Trump and Florida Governor Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump issues pardons for participants in the Jan. 6 riot at the U.S. Capitol
item.description: The pardons fulfilled Trump’s promise to release supporters who tried to help him overturn his election defeat four years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump grants sweeping pardon of Jan. 6 defendants, including rioters who violently attacked police
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned, commuted the prison sentences or vowed to dismiss the cases of all of the 1,500-plus people charged with crimes in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate passes immigrant detention bill that could be the first measure Trump signs into law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Fresh off President Donald Trump's inauguration, the Senate on Monday passed a bill that would require federal authorities to detain migrants accused of theft and violent crimes, the first measure he likely will sign into law and giving more weight to his plans to deport millions o
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Marco Rubio as secretary of state, giving Trump the first member of his Cabinet
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate quickly confirmed Marco Rubio as secretary of state Monday, voting unanimously to give President Donald Trump the first member of his new Cabinet on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Senate panel advances Noem nomination for Homeland Security secretary
item.description: A Senate committee cleared President Trump's nominee to lead the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) with a bipartisan vote, paving the way for a Senate floor vote. The Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee advanced South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R) by a 13-2 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order directing US withdrawal from the Paris climate agreement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday directing the United States to again withdraw from the landmark Paris climate agreement, dealing a blow to worldwide efforts to combat global warming and once again distancing the U.S. from its closest allies.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY and SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ratcliffe moves closer to CIA chief confirmation
item.description: John Ratcliffe took a step closer to being confirmed to lead the CIA on Monday as the Senate Intelligence Committee advanced his nomination. The panel advanced his nomination 14 to 3 shortly after President Trump was sworn into office. All three "no" votes were cast by Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio unanimously confirmed as secretary of State, becoming Trump's first Cabinet official
item.description: Rubio’s confirmation vote passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, 99-0. Rubio also was able to cast a vote for himself.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marco Rubio unanimously confirmed as secretary of State, becoming Trump's first Cabinet official
item.description: Rubio’s confirmation vote passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, 99-0. Rubio also was able to cast a vote for himself.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Republicans express optimism, state Democrat chair urges caution after inauguration
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) – Many Florida Republicans in the state legislature traveled to the nation’s capital for Inauguration Day events. Lawmakers like Sen. Rick Scott who works on Capitol Hill posted a video statement on X congratulating President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats congratulate, caution Trump as he starts 2nd term
item.description: Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation -- all Democrats -- congratulated and cautioned President Donald Trump as he began his second term Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: At Trump's second inaugural, a changed Washington and a full embrace from Republicans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump took the oath of office on Monday inside a tightly packed Capitol Rotunda, where he was surrounded by a very different Washington than he was eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Dawn of a New Era
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ramaswamy won't serve on Trump's government efficiency commission as he mulls run for Ohio governor
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency's first order of business was itself: It's now down to one leader.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's communication led to second presidency: Sam Donaldson
item.description: He suggested some of what Trump said highlighted the starkest departure from a past administration since Herbert Hoover and Franklin Roosevelt.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Newsom clash over wildfires, but California governor says he'll work with president
item.description: After President Trump criticizes California Gov. Newsom over the Los Angeles area wildfires during his inauguration address, the Democratic governor says he'll look for "common ground" with the new administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NJ governor sends letter to Trump seeking to end congestion pricing
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy sent a letter to President Donald Trump on Monday seeking to end congestion pricing in New York City. Congestion pricing went into effect on Jan. 5.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: What is an executive order? A look at Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: An incoming president signing a flurry of executive orders is standard practice. Executive orders allow a president to wield power without action from Congress. But there are also limits to what orders can achieve.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida officials react to Trump inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump was sworn in as the 47th President of the United States on Monday. Here's how Florida officials are responding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are all executive orders Trump is expected to sign on Day 1
item.description: An executive order is a signed statement about how the president wants the federal government to be managed.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Trump declares ‘Golden Age’ in America in inaugural address
item.description: President Trump on Monday declared it was the beginning of a new “Golden Age" in America, pledging to restore a country that he said had been decimated by the previous administration as he officially took the oath of office for his second White House term.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to sign executive orders live at Capital One Arena
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to sign executive orders in front of a live audience at Capital One Arena.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Inauguration Day Latest: Trump issues pardons for Jan. 6 rioters and signs more executive actions
item.description: Donald Trump has been sworn in as the 47th president of the United States, taking charge as Republicans claim unified control of Washington and set out to reshape the country’s institutions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Melania dons hat at Trump inauguration, struggles to kiss Trump
item.description: First lady Melania Trump wore a navy and ivory ensemble by American designer Adam Lippes for the inauguration ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier granted clemency by President Biden
item.description: The outgoing administration has released Leonard Peltier from prison. Numerous activists and tribal officials have requested the release of Peltier, whom they believe to be innocent of killing two FBI agents in 1975.
item.dc:creator: Allison Herrera and Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to issue executive orders defining sexes, ending DEI practices in government
item.description: President-elect Trump intends to issue executive orders on his first day in office that will recognize only two sexes, male and female, and aim to end diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices in the federal government, according to incoming White House officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams will attend Donald Trump’s inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams will attend President-elect Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday in Washington, D.C. Adams added the inauguration to his schedule Monday morning. He canceled existing appearances in New York City, including two celebrations of Martin Luther King Jr. Day.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11
